name: slider
title: Slider
description: A range input built on Base UI's Slider. One API covers a single thumb (value is a number) and a two-thumb range (value is [start, end]). Each thumb is a real role=slider input with aria-valuemin/max/now and full APG keyboard support. Value is conveyed by the fill length AND thumb position (not color alone), the track row and thumbs are 44px pointer targets, and an optional linked number input offers an exact-value alternative per USWDS guidance. Supports min/max/step, controlled and uncontrolled, optional tick marks, and Field wiring.
useWhen: Choosing a number within a known range where approximate selection is fine (volume, brightness, zoom)
Selecting a numeric range with two thumbs (price range, date span, min/max filter)
Offering a visual, draggable alternative to a bare number input while keeping the exact-value input available
avoidWhen: Precise or wide-open numeric entry with no meaningful bounds (use an Input type=number)
Choosing from a small set of discrete labelled options (use a radio-group or select)
Values where every increment matters and imprecise dragging would frustrate users (offer the number input instead)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to each thumb in turn; Shift+Tab moves back
Arrow Up/Right increases and Arrow Down/Left decreases by step (direction-aware in RTL)
Shift+Arrow, Page Up, and Page Down move by the larger step
Home jumps to the minimum and End to the maximum (bounded by the other thumb in range mode)
The optional exact-value number input accepts typed values and stays in sync
A disabled slider is removed from the tab order and cannot change
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (the pressable track row and each thumb hit area are 44px even though the visual rail and dot are smaller)
notes: Each thumb is a nested input type=range exposing role=slider with aria-valuemin/max/now and aria-valuetext; range thumbs are named '{label}, {minLabel}' and '{label}, {maxLabel}' via aria-labelledby.
Non-color redundancy: the filled indicator length AND the thumb position both convey the value, so it stays legible in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode where fill color is overridden.
The rail, indicator, and thumb dot keep visible borders so forced-colors mode paints boundaries; the focus ring hugs the thumb dot via the nested input's :focus-visible.
Physical thumb translate is handled by Base UI and mirrors in RTL; tick marks use logical inset so they mirror too.
Set valueInput to render a linked number field per thumb — an exact-value alternative for users who cannot operate a slider precisely (USWDS guidance).
Inside a Field the slider inherits id, aria-describedby, aria-invalid, required (visible indicator), and disabled; explicit props win.
